title: Fix the optional Value Check
prompt_md:
## Goal
This program uses `std::optional<int>` to represent a parse result that may fail, but it dereferences the optional without checking whether it holds a value. Fix the bug so the program correctly reports success or failure.

## Input
- Line 1: a non-negative integer (success) or the string `bad` (failure).

## Output
- If the input parses as a non-negative integer N, print `value: N`.
- If parsing fails, print `error: not a number`.

## Requirements
1. The program contains exactly one logic bug — fix only the condition check in main.
2. Do not change the parse function or the output format.
3. Edge cases: `0` is valid (print `value: 0`); `bad` must print the error line.

test_cases.label: Hidden: bad input triggers error path

title: Fix the ranges::filter Predicate
prompt_md:
## Goal
This program filters a list of integers and prints those strictly greater than a threshold, but the filter predicate has a logic bug — it keeps the wrong elements. Fix it.

## Input
- Line 1: integer T (the threshold).
- Line 2: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10).
- Lines 3..N+2: one integer per line.

## Output
Print all integers from the input list that are strictly greater than T, one per line, in their original order. If no integers satisfy the condition, print nothing.

## Requirements
1. The program contains exactly one bug — the lambda predicate uses the wrong comparison operator.
2. Fix only the comparison in the predicate; do not restructure the program.
3. Edge cases: T equal to some list values — only strictly greater pass; all values ≤ T → empty output.
